# PVSC Historical Lookup Checklist

**90 lots** need PVSC assessed-value lookup, across 6 years. Generated by `tax_sale/scripts/generate_pvsc_checklist.py` from the current dataset — re-run to refresh.

**Workflow** (full detail in [PVSC-LOOKUP-GUIDE.md](PVSC-LOOKUP-GUIDE.md)):

1. Open <https://www.pvsc.ca/find-assessment> → click **Search by Assessment Account Number**.
2. For each row below: paste the **AAN** into the search, solve the reCAPTCHA, read off assessed value, year built, lot acres, structure sqft from the result page.
3. Open the per-year CSV (generated below) and paste the numbers into the right row.
4. Cross-check the address PVSC shows against the **Civic Address** column — if they disagree by more than a house number, you may have hit a retired/renumbered AAN.

**Generate the per-year CSVs first** (these have the right column structure):

```bash
python3 -m tax_sale enrichment-template --year 2021 --out data/enrichment/pvsc-2021.csv
python3 -m tax_sale enrichment-template --year 2022 --out data/enrichment/pvsc-2022.csv
python3 -m tax_sale enrichment-template --year 2023 --out data/enrichment/pvsc-2023.csv
python3 -m tax_sale enrichment-template --year 2024 --out data/enrichment/pvsc-2024.csv
python3 -m tax_sale enrichment-template --year 2025 --out data/enrichment/pvsc-2025.csv
python3 -m tax_sale enrichment-template --year 2026 --out data/enrichment/pvsc-2026.csv
```

---

**After completing a year's lookups**, run:

```bash
python3 -m tax_sale stats   # `has_enrichment` count should reflect the lots you filled in
```

**To regenerate this checklist after adding enrichment data:**

```bash
python3 tax_sale/scripts/generate_pvsc_checklist.py --out tax_sale/PVSC-CHECKLIST.md
```

Already-enriched lots are filtered out, so the doc shrinks as you make progress.
